pages.full_text:
Cerisy la Foret (X)

+ blue stained glass, a fine sense of space light with high lantern + a lovely Madonna + Child (4
		We stopped at __Lessay__ which also has a fabulous abbey, very severe.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in colour photograph showing an aerial or elevated view of what appears to be Cerisy-la-Forêt Abbey and its surroundings. The label 'Cerisy la Foret (X)' is written vertically in the left margin in black ink — the '(X)' with a circled letter appears to be a photo reference number. The text below the photo is in pink/magenta ink: it continues a description of the abbey (blue stained glass, high lantern, Madonna and Child), with '(4' likely being a photo number reference. The diary then notes a stop at Lessay, which is underlined. No handwritten page number visible at top corners. Part of a Normandy, France trip; the exact year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
Coutances (13) facade of cathedral

COUTANCES La Cathédrale

Lessay

No gas. We brought along an empty cylinder. So no cup of tea....
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a colour postcard of Coutances Cathedral (La Cathédrale) with the printed caption 'COUTANCES La Cathédrale'. A handwritten vertical label in pink/magenta ink in the left margin reads 'Coutances (13) facade of cathedral' — '(13)' is a photo reference number. Below the postcard, on the lower left, is a hand-drawn sketch in pink ink of what appears to be an architectural capital or column detail, labelled 'Lessay' beneath it — this is an architectural detail sketch, not a route map. To the right of the sketch, the text reads: 'No gas. We brought along an empty cylinder. So no cup of tea....'. The top-right corner of the page is partially cut off; no clear handwritten page number is visible. Part of a Normandy, France trip; year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.
